tasks.title: Build crypto discovery, config, scheduling, and persistence foundation
tasks.description: The backend has the config, interval scheduling, fetcher, and ORM model foundation required to scan enabled chains every 60 seconds and store canonical launch state.

tasks.title: Add crypto repository, service orchestration, and runtime wiring
tasks.description: The backend can run scan cycles, tolerate per-chain failure, persist launches and snapshots, and expose scanner runtime state without affecting stock scheduling.

depends_on: Build crypto discovery, config, scheduling, and persistence foundation

tasks.title: Add crypto API contracts and endpoints
tasks.description: FastAPI exposes dedicated list, detail, refresh, and status endpoints for the crypto scanner using explicit partial-data semantics.

depends_on: Add crypto repository, service orchestration, and runtime wiring

tasks.title: Build the web scanner route, store, and detail experience
tasks.description: The web app exposes `/crypto-scanner` with launch feed rendering, filtering, sorting, auto-refresh, detail inspection, and shell navigation.
steps: cd apps/dsa-web && npm ci && npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test

depends_on: Add crypto API contracts and endpoints

tasks.title: Validate non-regression, partial-data behavior, and doc alignment
tasks.description: The bead ships with verification coverage for partial-data handling and confirmation that the stock lane and app shell still behave correctly.
steps: cd apps/dsa-web && npm ci && npm run lint && npm run build && npm run test

depends_on: "Build the web scanner route, store, and detail experience"

patterns: Pattern 1: `api/v1/endpoints/analysis.py` - Existing async request, status, and acceptance-response patterns are the closest backend model for crypto refresh and status endpoints.
Pattern 2: `src/repositories/stock_repo.py` - Existing repository structure is the nearest persistence pattern to mirror for crypto launch queries and upserts.
Pattern 3: `apps/dsa-web/src/hooks/useDashboardLifecycle.ts` - Existing polling plus visibility-refresh lifecycle is the right base pattern for a 60-second scanner page.
Pattern 4: `apps/dsa-web/src/stores/stockPoolStore.ts` - Existing Zustand store request sequencing and UI-state coordination are reusable for the crypto launch store.
keyFiles: `src/config.py` - Adds crypto config keys, enabled-chain normalization, and provider settings.
`src/scheduler.py` - Extends daily-only scheduling to support a 60-second interval scanner.
`main.py` - Wires scanner startup without depending on stock trading-day logic.
`api/v1/router.py` - Registers the new crypto API router.
`apps/dsa-web/src/components/layout/SidebarNav.tsx` - Adds scanner navigation entry in the existing shell.
`data_provider/base.py` - Boundary reference showing stock-specific normalization that must not be reused for crypto identifiers.
nonGoals: Replacing or deleting any existing stock-analysis workflow.
Persisted settings endpoints or UI for saved scanner defaults.
Alerts, watchlists, or notification delivery for launches.
AI review or per-minute LLM enrichment in the scanner loop.
Desktop-specific UX beyond existing web reuse in Electron.
Direct on-chain subscriptions or custom indexing infrastructure.
